name: shell_exec
description: Execute a shell command. Use this for exploration and discovery — listing directories (ls), finding files (find), checking if something exists (ls, which), searching content (grep). Always use shell_exec to explore unfamiliar paths before calling read_file. Allowlisted commands: git, ls, cat, grep, find, curl, node, python3, npm, echo, pwd, date, qmd, which, head, tail, wc, mkdir, cp, mv.

command.description: Shell command to run. Prefer ls/find when exploring; cat/head when you know the exact file.

working_dir.description: Working directory (optional, defaults to home directory)
